revision.text:
[[cdo:3 nguŏk 5 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 5 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月初五]] {{Lunisolar May}} '''May 5''' is the 5th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May|12=draft}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|12=draft}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Gendoku|de|元徳|ja|元徳|zh|元徳}} 3rd year / {{ill|Genkō|de|元弘|ja|元弘|zh|元弘}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|de|Julian calendar|ja|Julian calendar|zh|Julian calendar}} {{ill|1331|de|1331|ja|1331|zh|1331}} {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10}}) – In the rebellion known as {{ill|Genkō no Ran|de|元弘の乱|ja|元弘の乱|zh|元弘の乱}}, the reattempted anti-bakufu plot of Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|de|後醍醐天皇|ja|後醍醐天皇|zh|後醍醐天皇}} was leaked to the Ashikaga Shogunate, resulting in the arrest of figures such as {{ill|Hino Toshiki|de|日野俊基|ja|日野俊基|zh|日野俊基}}. The emperor then took refuge on 【Oki Province†Oki_Province】. * {{ill|Genna|de|Genna|ja|元和|zh|Genna}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1615|de|1615|ja|1615|zh|1615}} {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|de|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|zh|Tokugawa Ieyasu}} set out from Kyoto (from Nijō Castle) in preparation for the summer siege of Osaka. * {{ill|Tenmei|de|Tenmei|ja|天保|zh|Tenmei}} 6th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1786|de|1786|ja|1786|zh|1786}} June 1) – Under orders from the Tokugawa shogunate, the official {{ill|Mogami Tokunai|de|最上徳内|ja|最上徳内|zh|最上徳内}} landed on Ezo (specifically, on Etoroshima, also known as Etoroshima in Oki Province).
== Births == * {{ill|Jōgan|de|Jōgan|ja|Jōgan|zh|Jōgan}} 9th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|de|Julian calendar|ja|Julian calendar|zh|Julian calendar}} {{ill|867|de|867|ja|867|zh|867}} {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10}}) – {{ill|Uta Tennō|de|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇|zh|宇多天皇}}, the 59th Emperor of Japan (died [[931]]). * {{ill|Keichō|de|Keichō|ja|慶長|zh|Keichō}} 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1604|de|1604|ja|1604|zh|1604}} {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2}}) – {{ill|Uesugi Sadakatsu|de|上杉定勝|ja|上杉定勝|zh|上杉定勝}}, the 2nd lord of the Yonezawa Domain (died [[1645]]). * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 19th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1642|de|1642|ja|1642|zh|1642}} {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2}}) – {{ill|Yamaguchi Sodo|de|山口素堂|ja|山口素堂|zh|山口素堂}}, a haiku poet (died [[1716]]). * {{ill|Enkyō|ja|延享|12=draft}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1746|de|1746|ja|1746|zh|1746}} {{ill|June 23|de|June 23|ja|June 23|zh|June 23}}) – {{ill|Hanawa Hokeiichi|de|塙保己一|ja|塙保己一|zh|塙保己一}}, a scholar of Japanese literature (died [[1821]]). * {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|12=draft}} 13rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1763|de|1763|ja|1763|zh|1763}} {{ill|June 15|de|June 15|ja|June 15|zh|June 15}}) – {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|de|小林一茶|ja|小林一茶|zh|小林一茶}}, a haiku poet (died [[1827]]). * {{ill|Bunka|ja|文政|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1804|de|1804|ja|1804|zh|1804}} {{ill|June 12|de|June 12|ja|June 12|zh|June 12}}) – {{ill|Takano Nagahide|de|高野長英|ja|高野長英|zh|高野長英}}, a Rangaku scholar (died [[1850]]). * {{ill|Bunka|ja|文政|12=draft}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1828|de|1828|ja|1828|zh|1828}} {{ill|June 16|de|June 16|ja|June 16|zh|June 16}}) – {{ill|Hirose Sahei|de|広瀬宰平|ja|広瀬宰平|zh|広瀬宰平}}, a businessperson (died [[1914]]). * {{ill|Bunka|ja|文政|12=draft}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1829|de|1829|ja|1829|zh|1829}} {{ill|June 6|de|June 6|ja|June 6|zh|June 6}}) – {{ill|Honinbō Shūsaku|de|本因坊秀策|ja|本因坊秀策|zh|本因坊秀策}}, a professional Go player (died [[1862]]). * {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 6th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1835|de|1835|ja|1835|zh|1835}} {{ill|May 31|de|May 31|ja|May 31|zh|May 31}}) – {{ill|Hijikata Toshizō|de|土方歳三|ja|土方歳三|zh|土方歳三}}, vice-commander of the Shinsengumi (died [[1869]]).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|12=draft}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1680|de|1680|ja|1680|zh|1680}} {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1}}) – {{ill|Hayashi Gahō|de|林鵞峰|ja|林鵞峰|zh|林鵞峰}} (Shunsai), a Zhu Xi scholar and son of Hayashi Rinsan (born {{ill|1618|de|1618|ja|1618|zh|1618}}). * {{ill|Genji|ja|元治|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1864|de|1864|ja|1864|zh|1864}} {{ill|June 8|de|June 8|ja|June 8|zh|June 8}}) – {{ill|Reisui Iguyasu|de|冷泉為恭|ja|冷泉為恭|zh|冷泉為恭}}, an imperial court painter.
== Commemorations == * {{ill|Duanwu|de|Duanwu|ja|端午|zh|端午}} – The Dragon Boat Festival is celebrated. * Duanwu Festival (端午節) is observed in Taiwan. <ref>{{Cite web|url=http://jp.taiwantoday.tw/news.php?unit=151&post=115869|title=TAIWAN TODAY日本語版からのお知らせ|publisher=Taiwan Today|accessdate=2017-05-26}}</ref>
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|4|5|6|4|5|6|5|0505}}

revision.text:
[[cdo:3 nguŏk 5 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 5 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月初五]] {{Lunisolar May}} '''May 5''' is the 5th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May|12=draft}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|12=draft}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Gendoku|de|元徳|ja|元徳|zh|元徳}} 3rd year / {{ill|Genkō|de|元弘|ja|元弘|zh|元弘}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|de|Julian calendar|ja|Julian calendar|zh|Julian calendar}} {{ill|1331|de|1331|ja|1331|zh|1331}} {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10}}) – In the rebellion known as {{ill|Genkō no Ran|de|元弘の乱|ja|元弘の乱|zh|元弘の乱}}, the reattempted anti-bakufu plot of Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|de|後醍醐天皇|ja|後醍醐天皇|zh|後醍醐天皇}} was leaked to the Ashikaga Shogunate, resulting in the arrest of figures such as {{ill|Hino Toshiki|de|日野俊基|ja|日野俊基|zh|日野俊基}}. The emperor then took refuge on 【Oki Province†Oki_Province】. * {{ill|Genna|de|Genna|ja|元和|zh|Genna}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1615|de|1615|ja|1615|zh|1615}} {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|de|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|zh|Tokugawa Ieyasu}} set out from Kyoto (from Nijō Castle) in preparation for the summer siege of Osaka. * {{ill|Tenmei|de|Tenmei|ja|天保|zh|Tenmei}} 6th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1786|de|1786|ja|1786|zh|1786}} June 1) – Under orders from the Tokugawa shogunate, the official {{ill|Mogami Tokunai|de|最上徳内|ja|最上徳内|zh|最上徳内}} landed on Ezo (specifically, on Etoroshima, also known as Etoroshima in Oki Province).
== Births == * {{ill|Jōgan|de|Jōgan|ja|Jōgan|zh|Jōgan}} 9th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|de|Julian calendar|ja|Julian calendar|zh|Julian calendar}} {{ill|867|de|867|ja|867|zh|867}} {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10}}) – {{ill|Uta Tennō|de|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇|zh|宇多天皇}}, the 59th Emperor of Japan (died [[931]]). * {{ill|Keichō|de|Keichō|ja|慶長|zh|Keichō}} 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1604|de|1604|ja|1604|zh|1604}} {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2}}) – {{ill|Uesugi Sadakatsu|de|上杉定勝|ja|上杉定勝|zh|上杉定勝}}, the 2nd lord of the Yonezawa Domain (died [[1645]]). * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 19th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1642|de|1642|ja|1642|zh|1642}} {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2}}) – {{ill|Yamaguchi Sodo|de|山口素堂|ja|山口素堂|zh|山口素堂}}, a haiku poet (died [[1716]]). * 23|ja|June 23|zh|June 23}}) – {{ill|Hanawa Hokeiichi|de|塙保己一|ja|塙保己一|zh|塙保己一}}, a scholar of Japanese literature (died [[1821]]). * {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|12=draft}} 13rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1763|de|1763|ja|1763|zh|1763}} {{ill|June 15|de|June 15|ja|June 15|zh|June 15}}) – {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|de|小林一茶|ja|小林一茶|zh|小林一茶}}, a haiku poet (died [[1827]]). * {{ill|Bunka|ja|文政|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1829|de|1829|ja|1829|zh|1829}} {{ill|June 6|de|June 6|ja|June 6|zh|June 6}}) – {{ill|Honinbō Shūsaku|de|本因坊秀策|ja|本因坊秀策|zh|本因坊秀策}}, a professional Go player (died [[1862]]). * {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 6th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1835|de|1835|ja|1835|zh|1835}} {{ill|May 31|de|May 31|ja|May 31|zh|May 31}}) – {{ill|Hijikata Toshizō|de|土方歳三|ja|土方歳三|zh|土方歳三}}, vice-commander of the Shinsengumi (died [[1869]]).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|12=draft}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1680|de|1680|ja|1680|zh|1680}} {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1}}) – {{ill|Hayashi Gahō|de|林鵞峰|ja|林鵞峰|zh|林鵞峰}} (Shunsai), a Zhu Xi scholar and son of Hayashi Rinsan (born {{ill|1618|de|1618|ja|1618|zh|1618}}). * {{ill|Genji|ja|元治|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1864|de|1864|ja|1864|zh|1864}} {{ill|June 8|de|June 8|ja|June 8|zh|June 8}}) – {{ill|Reisui Iguyasu|de|冷泉為恭|ja|冷泉為恭|zh|冷泉為恭}}, an imperial court painter.
== Commemorations == * {{ill|Duanwu|de|Duanwu|ja|端午|zh|端午}} – The Dragon Boat Festival is celebrated. * Duanwu Festival (端午節) is observed in Taiwan. <ref>{{Cite web|url=http://jp.taiwantoday.tw/news.php?unit=151&post=115869|title=TAIWAN TODAY日本語版からのお知らせ|publisher=Taiwan Today|accessdate=2017-05-26}}</ref>

[[cdo:3 nguŏk 5 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 5 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月初五]] {{Lunisolar May}} '''May 5''' is the 5th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May|12=draft}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|12=draft}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Gendoku|de|元徳|ja|元徳|zh|元徳}} 3rd year / {{ill|Genkō|de|元弘|ja|元弘|zh|元弘}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|de|Julian calendar|ja|Julian calendar|zh|Julian calendar}} {{ill|1331|de|1331|ja|1331|zh|1331}} {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10}}) – In the rebellion known as {{ill|Genkō no Ran|de|元弘の乱|ja|元弘の乱|zh|元弘の乱}}, the reattempted anti-bakufu plot of Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|de|後醍醐天皇|ja|後醍醐天皇|zh|後醍醐天皇}} was leaked to the Ashikaga Shogunate, resulting in the arrest of figures such as {{ill|Hino Toshiki|de|日野俊基|ja|日野俊基|zh|日野俊基}}. The emperor then took refuge on 【Oki Province†Oki_Province】. * {{ill|Genna|de|Genna|ja|元和|zh|Genna}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1615|de|1615|ja|1615|zh|1615}} {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|de|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|zh|Tokugawa Ieyasu}} set out from Kyoto (from Nijō Castle) in preparation for the summer siege of Osaka. * {{ill|Tenmei|de|Tenmei|ja|天保|zh|Tenmei}} 6th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1786|de|1786|ja|1786|zh|1786}} June 1) – Under orders from the Tokugawa shogunate, the official {{ill|Mogami Tokunai|de|最上徳内|ja|最上徳内|zh|最上徳内}} landed on Ezo (specifically, on Etoroshima, also known as Etoroshima in Oki Province).
== Births == * {{ill|Jōgan|de|Jōgan|ja|Jōgan|zh|Jōgan}} 9th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|de|Julian calendar|ja|Julian calendar|zh|Julian calendar}} {{ill|867|de|867|ja|867|zh|867}} {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10}}) – {{ill|Uta Tennō|de|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇|zh|宇多天皇}}, the 59th Emperor of Japan (died [[931]]). * {{ill|Keichō|de|Keichō|ja|慶長|zh|Keichō}} 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1604|de|1604|ja|1604|zh|1604}} {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2}}) – {{ill|Uesugi Sadakatsu|de|上杉定勝|ja|上杉定勝|zh|上杉定勝}}, the 2nd lord of the Yonezawa Domain (died [[1645]]). * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 19th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1642|de|1642|ja|1642|zh|1642}} {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2}}) – {{ill|Yamaguchi Sodo|de|山口素堂|ja|山口素堂|zh|山口素堂}}, a haiku poet (died [[1716]]). * {{ill|Enkyō|ja|延享|12=draft}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1746|de|1746|ja|1746|zh|1746}} {{ill|June 23|de|June 23|ja|June 23|zh|June 23}}) – {{ill|Hanawa Hokeiichi|de|塙保己一|ja|塙保己一|zh|塙保己一}}, a scholar of Japanese literature (died [[1821]]). * {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|12=draft}} 13rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1763|de|1763|ja|1763|zh|1763}} {{ill|June 15|de|June 15|ja|June 15|zh|June 15}}) – {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|de|小林一茶|ja|小林一茶|zh|小林一茶}}, a haiku poet (died [[1827]]). * {{ill|Bunka|ja|文政|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1804|de|1804|ja|1804|zh|1804}} {{ill|June 12|de|June 12|ja|June 12|zh|June 12}}) – {{ill|Takano Nagahide|de|高野長英|ja|高野長英|zh|高野長英}}, a Rangaku scholar (died [[1850]]). * {{ill|Bunka|ja|文政|12=draft}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1828|de|1828|ja|1828|zh|1828}} {{ill|June 16|de|June 16|ja|June 16|zh|June 16}}) – {{ill|Hirose Sahei|de|広瀬宰平|ja|広瀬宰平|zh|広瀬宰平}}, a businessperson (died [[1914]]). * {{ill|Bunka|ja|文政|12=draft}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1829|de|1829|ja|1829|zh|1829}} {{ill|June 6|de|June 6|ja|June 6|zh|June 6}}) – {{ill|Honinbō Shūsaku|de|本因坊秀策|ja|本因坊秀策|zh|本因坊秀策}}, a professional Go player (died [[1862]]). * {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 6th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1835|de|1835|ja|1835|zh|1835}} {{ill|May 31|de|May 31|ja|May 31|zh|May 31}}) – {{ill|Hijikata Toshizō|de|土方歳三|ja|土方歳三|zh|土方歳三}}, vice-commander of the Shinsengumi (died [[1869]]).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|12=draft}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1680|de|1680|ja|1680|zh|1680}} {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1}}) – {{ill|Hayashi Gahō|de|林鵞峰|ja|林鵞峰|zh|林鵞峰}} (Shunsai), a Zhu Xi scholar and son of Hayashi Rinsan (born {{ill|1618|de|1618|ja|1618|zh|1618}}). * {{ill|Genji|ja|元治|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1864|de|1864|ja|1864|zh|1864}} {{ill|June 8|de|June 8|ja|June 8|zh|June 8}}) – {{ill|Reisui Iguyasu|de|冷泉為恭|ja|冷泉為恭|zh|冷泉為恭}}, an imperial court painter.
== Commemorations == * {{ill|Duanwu|de|Duanwu|ja|端午|zh|端午}} – The Dragon Boat Festival is celebrated. * Duanwu Festival (端午節) is observed in Taiwan. <ref>{{Cite web|url=http://jp.taiwantoday.tw/news.php?unit=151&post=115869|title=TAIWAN TODAY日本語版からのお知らせ|publisher=Taiwan Today|accessdate=2017-05-26}}</ref>

== Births == * {{ill|Jōgan|de|Jōgan|ja|Jōgan|zh|Jōgan|12=draft}} 9th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|de|Julian calendar|ja|Julian calendar|zh|Julian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|867|de|867|ja|867|zh|867|12=draft}} {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Uta Tennō|de|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇|zh|宇多天皇|12=draft}}, the 59th Emperor of Japan (died [[931]]). * {{ill|Keichō|de|Keichō|ja|慶長|zh|Keichō|12=draft}} 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1604|de|1604|ja|1604|zh|1604|12=draft}} {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Uesugi Sadakatsu|de|上杉定勝|ja|上杉定勝|zh|上杉定勝|12=draft}}, the 2nd lord of the Yonezawa Domain (died [[1645]]). * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 19th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1642|de|1642|ja|1642|zh|1642|12=draft}} {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Yamaguchi Sodo|de|山口素堂|ja|山口素堂|zh|山口素堂|12=draft}}, a haiku poet (died [[1716]]). * {{ill|Enkyō|ja|延享|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1746|de|1746|ja|1746|zh|1746|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|de|June 23|ja|June 23|zh|June 23|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Hanawa Hokeiichi|de|塙保己一|ja|塙保己一|zh|塙保己一|12=draft}}, a scholar of Japanese literature (died [[1821]]). * {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 13rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1763|de|1763|ja|1763|zh|1763|12=draft}} {{ill|June 15|de|June 15|ja|June 15|zh|June 15|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|de|小林一茶|ja|小林一茶|zh|小林一茶|12=draft}}, a haiku poet (died [[1827]]). * {{ill|Bunka|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1804|de|1804|ja|1804|zh|1804|12=draft}} {{ill|June 12|de|June 12|ja|June 12|zh|June 12|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Takano Nagahide|de|高野長英|ja|高野長英|zh|高野長英|12=draft}}, a Rangaku scholar (died [[1850]]). * {{ill|Bunka|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1828|de|1828|ja|1828|zh|1828|12=draft}} {{ill|June 16|de|June 16|ja|June 16|zh|June 16|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Hirose Sahei|de|広瀬宰平|ja|広瀬宰平|zh|広瀬宰平|12=draft}}, a businessperson (died [[1914]]). * {{ill|Bunka|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1829|de|1829|ja|1829|zh|1829|12=draft}} {{ill|June 6|de|June 6|ja|June 6|zh|June 6|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Honinbō Shūsaku|de|本因坊秀策|ja|本因坊秀策|zh|本因坊秀策|12=draft}}, a professional Go player (died [[1862]]). * {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft|12=draft}} 6th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1835|de|1835|ja|1835|zh|1835|12=draft}} {{ill|May 31|de|May 31|ja|May 31|zh|May 31|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Hijikata Toshizō|de|土方歳三|ja|土方歳三|zh|土方歳三|12=draft}}, vice-commander of the Shinsengumi (died [[1869]]).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|12=draft|12=draft}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1680|de|1680|ja|1680|zh|1680|12=draft}} {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Hayashi Gahō|de|林鵞峰|ja|林鵞峰|zh|林鵞峰|12=draft}} (Shunsai), a Zhu Xi scholar and son of Hayashi Rinsan (born {{ill|1618|de|1618|ja|1618|zh|1618|12=draft}}). * {{ill|Genji|ja|元治|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1864|de|1864|ja|1864|zh|1864|12=draft}} {{ill|June 8|de|June 8|ja|June 8|zh|June 8|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Reisui Iguyasu|de|冷泉為恭|ja|冷泉為恭|zh|冷泉為恭|12=draft}}, an imperial court painter.
== Commemorations == * {{ill|Duanwu|de|Duanwu|ja|端午|zh|端午|12=draft}} – The Dragon Boat Festival is celebrated. * Duanwu Festival (端午節) is observed in Taiwan. <ref>{{Cite web|url=http://jp.taiwantoday.tw/news.php?unit=151&post=115869|title=TAIWAN TODAY日本語版からのお知らせ|publisher=Taiwan Today|accessdate=2017-05-26}}</ref>
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|4|5|6|4|5|6|5|0505}}

[[cdo:3 nguŏk 5 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 5 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月初五]] {{Lunisolar May}} '''May 5''' is the 5th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May|12=draft|12=draft}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Gendoku|de|元徳|ja|元徳|zh|元徳|12=draft}} 3rd year / {{ill|Genkō|de|元弘|ja|元弘|zh|元弘|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|de|Julian calendar|ja|Julian calendar|zh|Julian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1331|de|1331|ja|1331|zh|1331|12=draft}} {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|12=draft}}) – In the rebellion known as {{ill|Genkō no Ran|de|元弘の乱|ja|元弘の乱|zh|元弘の乱|12=draft}}, the reattempted anti-bakufu plot of Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|de|後醍醐天皇|ja|後醍醐天皇|zh|後醍醐天皇|12=draft}} was leaked to the Ashikaga Shogunate, resulting in the arrest of figures such as {{ill|Hino Toshiki|de|日野俊基|ja|日野俊基|zh|日野俊基|12=draft}}. The emperor then took refuge on 【Oki Province†Oki_Province】. * {{ill|Genna|de|Genna|ja|元和|zh|Genna|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1615|de|1615|ja|1615|zh|1615|12=draft}} {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|de|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|zh|Tokugawa Ieyasu|12=draft}} set out from Kyoto (from Nijō Castle) in preparation for the summer siege of Osaka. * {{ill|Tenmei|de|Tenmei|ja|天保|zh|Tenmei|12=draft}} 6th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1786|de|1786|ja|1786|zh|1786|12=draft}} June 1) – Under orders from the Tokugawa shogunate, the official {{ill|Mogami Tokunai|de|最上徳内|ja|最上徳内|zh|最上徳内|12=draft}} landed on Ezo (specifically, on Etoroshima, also known as Etoroshima in Oki Province).
== Births == * {{ill|Jōgan|de|Jōgan|ja|Jōgan|zh|Jōgan|12=draft}} 9th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|de|Julian calendar|ja|Julian calendar|zh|Julian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|867|de|867|ja|867|zh|867|12=draft}} {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Uta Tennō|de|宇多天皇|ja|宇多天皇|zh|宇多天皇|12=draft}}, the 59th Emperor of Japan (died [[931]]). * {{ill|Keichō|de|Keichō|ja|慶長|zh|Keichō|12=draft}} 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1604|de|1604|ja|1604|zh|1604|12=draft}} {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Uesugi Sadakatsu|de|上杉定勝|ja|上杉定勝|zh|上杉定勝|12=draft}}, the 2nd lord of the Yonezawa Domain (died [[1645]]). * {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 19th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1642|de|1642|ja|1642|zh|1642|12=draft}} {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Yamaguchi Sodo|de|山口素堂|ja|山口素堂|zh|山口素堂|12=draft}}, a haiku poet (died [[1716]]). * {{ill|Enkyō|ja|延享|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1746|de|1746|ja|1746|zh|1746|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|de|June 23|ja|June 23|zh|June 23|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Hanawa Hokeiichi|de|塙保己一|ja|塙保己一|zh|塙保己一|12=draft}}, a scholar of Japanese literature (died [[1821]]). * {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 13rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1763|de|1763|ja|1763|zh|1763|12=draft}} {{ill|June 15|de|June 15|ja|June 15|zh|June 15|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|de|小林一茶|ja|小林一茶|zh|小林一茶|12=draft}}, a haiku poet (died [[1827]]). * {{ill|Bunka|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1804|de|1804|ja|1804|zh|1804|12=draft}} {{ill|June 12|de|June 12|ja|June 12|zh|June 12|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Takano Nagahide|de|高野長英|ja|高野長英|zh|高野長英|12=draft}}, a Rangaku scholar (died [[1850]]). * {{ill|Bunka|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1828|de|1828|ja|1828|zh|1828|12=draft}} {{ill|June 16|de|June 16|ja|June 16|zh|June 16|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Hirose Sahei|de|広瀬宰平|ja|広瀬宰平|zh|広瀬宰平|12=draft}}, a businessperson (died [[1914]]). * {{ill|Bunka|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian Shūsaku|de|本因坊秀策|ja|本因坊秀策|zh|本因坊秀策|12=draft}}, a professional Go player (died [[1862]]). * {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft|12=draft}} 6th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1835|de|1835|ja|1835|zh|1835|12=draft}} {{ill|May 31|de|May 31|ja|May 31|zh|May 31|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Hijikata Toshizō|de|土方歳三|ja|土方歳三|zh|土方歳三|12=draft}}, vice-commander of the Shinsengumi (died [[1869]]).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|12=draft|12=draft}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1680|de|1680|ja|1680|zh|1680|12=draft}} {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Hayashi Gahō|de|林鵞峰|ja|林鵞峰|zh|林鵞峰|12=draft}} (Shunsai), a Zhu Xi scholar and son of Hayashi Rinsan (born {{ill|1618|de|1618|ja|1618|zh|1618|12=draft}}). * {{ill|Genji|ja|元治|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1864|de|1864|ja|1864|zh|1864|12=draft}} {{ill|June 8|de|June 8|ja|June 8|zh|June 8|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Reisui Iguyasu|de|冷泉為恭|ja|冷泉為恭|zh|冷泉為恭|12=draft}}, an imperial court painter.
== Commemorations == * {{ill|Duanwu|de|Duanwu|ja|端午|zh|端午|12=draft}} – The Dragon Boat Festival is celebrated. * Duanwu Festival (端午節) is observed in Taiwan. <ref>{{Cite web|url=http://jp.taiwantoday.tw/news.php?unit=151&post=115869|title=TAIWAN TODAY日本語版からのお知らせ|publisher=Taiwan Today|accessdate=2017-05-26}}</ref>

== Events == * {{ill|Gendoku|de|元徳|ja|元徳|zh|元徳|12=draft}} 3rd year / {{ill|Genkō|de|元弘|ja|元弘|zh|元弘|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|de|Julian calendar|ja|Julian calendar|zh|Julian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1331|de|1331|ja|1331|zh|1331|12=draft}} {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|12=draft}}) – In the rebellion known as {{ill|Genkō no Ran|de|元弘の乱|ja|元弘の乱|zh|元弘の乱|12=draft}}, the reattempted anti-bakufu plot of Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|de|後醍醐天皇|ja|後醍醐天皇|zh|後醍醐天皇|12=draft}} was leaked to the Ashikaga Shogunate, resulting in the arrest of figures such as {{ill|Hino Toshiki|de|日野俊基|ja|日野俊基|zh|日野俊基|12=draft}}. The emperor then took refuge on 【Oki Province†Oki_Province】. * {{ill|Genna|de|Genna|ja|元和|zh|Genna|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1615|de|1615|ja|1615|zh|1615|12=draft}} {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|de|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|zh|Tokugawa Ieyasu|12=draft}} set out from Kyoto (from Nijō Castle) in preparation for the summer siege of Osaka. * {{ill|Tenmei|de|Tenmei|ja|天保|zh|Tenmei|12=draft}} 6th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|de|Gregorian calendar|ja|Gregorian calendar|zh|Gregorian calendar|12=draft}} {{ill|1786|de|1786|ja|1786|zh|1786|12=draft}} June 1) – Under orders from the Tokugawa shogunate, the official {{ill|Mogami Tokunai|de|最上徳内|ja|最上徳内|zh|最上徳内|12=draft}} landed on Ezo (specifically, on Etoroshima, also known as Etoroshima in Oki Province).

revision.text:
{{旧暦5月}} '''旧暦5月5日'''（きゅうれきごがついつか）は、[[5月 (旧暦)|旧暦5月]]の5日目である。六曜は[[六曜#先負|先負]]である。
== できごと == * [[元徳]]3年/[[元弘]]元年（[[ユリウス暦]][[1331年]][[6月10日]]） - [[元弘の乱]]{{要出典|date=2021-04}}。[[後醍醐天皇]]の再度の倒幕計画が[[室町幕府]]に漏洩し[[日野俊基]]らを逮捕。天皇は笠置山に籠城したが翌年[[隠岐国|隠岐]]に流される * [[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1615年]][[6月1日]]） - [[徳川家康]]が[[大坂の陣#大坂夏の陣|大坂夏の陣]]の為に京都・[[二条城]]を出陣 * [[天明]]6年（グレゴリオ暦[[1786年]]6月1日） - 江戸幕府の命を受けた[[最上徳内]]が[[択捉島]]に上陸
== 誕生日 == * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（ユリウス暦[[867年]][[6月10日]]） - [[宇多天皇]]、59代[[天皇]]（+ [[931年]]） * [[慶長]]9年（グレゴリオ暦[[1604年]][[6月2日]]） - [[上杉定勝]]、第2代[[米沢藩]]主（+ [[1645年]]） * [[寛永]]19年（グレゴリオ暦[[1642年]][[6月2日]]） - [[山口素堂]]、[[俳人]]（+ [[1716年]]） * [[延享]]3年（グレゴリオ暦[[1746年]][[6月23日]]） - [[塙保己一]]、[[国文学者]]（+ [[1821年]]） * [[宝暦]]13年（グレゴリオ暦[[1763年]][[6月15日]]） - [[小林一茶]]、[[俳諧師]]（+ [[1827年]]） * [[文化 (元号)|文化]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1804年]][[6月12日]]） - [[高野長英]]、[[蘭学者]]（+ [[1850年]]） * [[文政]]11年（グレゴリオ暦[[1828年]][[6月16日]]） - [[広瀬宰平]]、[[実業家]]（+ [[1914年]]） * [[文政]]12年（グレゴリオ暦[[1829年]][[6月6日]]） - [[本因坊秀策]]、[[棋士 (囲碁)|囲碁棋士]]（+ [[1862年]]） * [[天保]]6年（グレゴリオ暦[[1835年]][[5月31日]]） - [[土方歳三]]、[[新選組]]副長（+ [[1869年]]）
== 忌日 == * [[延宝]]8年（グレゴリオ暦[[1680年]][[6月1日]]） - [[林鵞峰]]（春斎）、[[朱子学者]]・[[林羅山]]の子（* [[1618年]]） *[[元治]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1864年]][[6月8日]]） - [[冷泉為恭]]、宮廷絵師
== 記念日・年中行事 == * [[端午]] * 端午節（{{TWN}}）<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date=2017-05-26 |url=http://jp.taiwantoday.tw/news.php?unit=151&post=115869 |title=TAIWAN TODAY日本語版からのお知らせ |publisher=Taiwan Today |accessdate=2017-05-26}}</ref>
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{旧暦360日|5|4|5|6|4|5|6|5|0505}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[5月5日 (旧暦)]] to [[May 5 (Lunisolar)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月初五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 5일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 5''' is the 5th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Gendoku|de|元徳|ja|元徳|zh|元徳|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gendoku|qq=}} 3rd year / {{ill|Genkō|de|元弘|ja|元弘|zh|元弘|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō|qq=}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|de|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|zh|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|1331|de|1331|ja|1331|zh|1331|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1331|qq=}} {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 10|qq=}}) – In the rebellion known as {{ill|Genkō no Ran|de|元弘の乱|ja|元弘の乱|zh|元弘の乱|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō no Ran|qq=}}, the reattempted anti-bakufu plot of Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|de|後醍醐天皇|ja|後醍醐天皇|zh|後醍醐天皇|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Daigo|qq=}} was leaked to the Ashikaga Shogunate, resulting in the arrest of figures such as {{ill|Hino Toshiki|de|日野俊基|ja|日野俊基|zh|日野俊基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Toshiki|qq=}}. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月初五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 5일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 5''' is the 5th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Gendoku|de|元徳|ja|元徳|zh|元徳|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gendoku|qq=}} 3rd year / {{ill|Genkō|de|元弘|ja|元弘|zh|元弘|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō|qq=}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|de|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|zh|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|1331|de|1331|ja|1331|zh|1331|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1331|qq=}} {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 10|qq=}}) – In the rebellion known as {{ill|Genkō no Ran|de|元弘の乱|ja|元弘の乱|zh|元弘の乱|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkō no Ran|qq=}}, the reattempted anti-bakufu plot of Emperor {{ill|Go-Daigo|de|後醍醐天皇|ja|後醍醐天皇|zh|後醍醐天皇|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Daigo|qq=}} was leaked to the Ashikaga Shogunate, resulting in the arrest of figures such as {{ill|Hino Toshiki|de|日野俊基|ja|日野俊基|zh|日野俊基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Toshiki|qq=}}. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月初五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 5일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 5''' is the 5th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=}}.

{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月初五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 5일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 5''' is the 5th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=}}.

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月初五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 5일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 5''' is the 5th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=}}.

{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月初五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 5일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 5''' is the 5th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 5 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[hak:5-ngie̍t 5-ngit (nùng-la̍k)]][[ja:5月5日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 5일]][[zh_min_nan:5 goe̍h 5 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:五月初五]][[zh:五月初五]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q839531]] Wikidata name: 5th day of the 5th month in the Chinese lunar calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|May 5|qq=May 5}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月初五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 5일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 5''' is the 5th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|qid=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Gentoku|Gendoku]] 3rd year / [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1331|1331]] {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 10|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – In the rebellion known as [[:en:Genkō War|Genkō no Ran]], the reattempted anti-bakufu plot of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]] was leaked to the Ashikaga Shogunate, resulting in the arrest of figures such as {{ill|Hino Toshiki|de|日野俊基|ja|日野俊基|zh|日野俊基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Toshiki|qq=|1=Hino Toshimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510567}}. The emperor then took refuge on 【Oki Province†Oki_Province】. * [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1615|1615]] {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] set out from Kyoto (from Nijō Castle) in preparation for the summer siege of Osaka. * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenmei]] 6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1786|1786]] June 1) – Under orders from the Tokugawa shogunate, the official [[:en:Mogami Tokunai|Mogami Tokunai]] landed on Ezo (specifically, on Etoroshima, also known as Etoroshima in Oki Province). == Births == * [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 9th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:867|867]] {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 10|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Emperor Uda|Uta Tennō]], the 59th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:931|931]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – {{ill|Uesugi Sadakatsu|de|上杉定勝|ja|上杉定勝|zh|上杉定勝|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uesugi Sadakatsu|qq=|1=Uesugi Sadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514762}}, the 2nd lord of the Yonezawa Domain (died [[:en:1645|1645]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1642|1642]] {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – {{ill|Yamaguchi Sodo|de|山口素堂|ja|山口素堂|zh|山口素堂|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Sodo|qq=|1=Yamaguchi Sodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467412}}, a haiku poet (died [[:en:1716|1716]]). * [[:en:Enkyō (Edo period)|Enkyō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1746|1746]] {{ill|June 23|de|June 23|ja|June 23|zh|June 23|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 23|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hanawa Hokiichi|Hanawa Hokeiichi]], a scholar of Japanese literature (died [[:en:1821|1821]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 13rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1763|1763]] {{ill|June 15|de|June 15|ja|June 15|zh|June 15|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 15|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Kobayashi Issa|Kobayashi Issa]], a haiku poet (died [[:en:1827|1827]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunka]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1804|1804]] {{ill|June 12|de|June 12|ja|June 12|zh|June 12|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 12|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Takano Chōei|Takano Nagahide]], a Rangaku scholar (died [[:en:1850|1850]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunka]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1828|1828]] {{ill|June 16|de|June 16|ja|June 16|zh|June 16|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 16|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Saihei Hirose|Hirose Sahei]], a businessperson (died [[:en:1914|1914]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunka]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] {{ill|June 6|de|June 6|ja|June 6|zh|June 6|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hon'inbō Shūsaku|Honinbō Shūsaku]], a professional Go player (died [[:en:1862|1862]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1835|1835]] {{ill|May 31|de|May 31|ja|May 31|zh|May 31|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 31|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hijikata Toshizō|Hijikata Toshizō]], vice-commander of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1869|1869]]). == Deaths == * [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1680|1680]] {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Gahō]] (Shunsai), a Zhu Xi scholar and son of Hayashi Rinsan (born [[:en:1618|1618]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] {{ill|June 8|de|June 8|ja|June 8|zh|June 8|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 8|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Reizei Tamechika|Reisui Iguyasu]], an imperial court painter. == Commemorations == * [[:en:Dragon Boat Festival|Dragon Boat Festival]] – The Dragon Boat Festival is celebrated. * Duanwu Festival (端午節) is observed in Taiwan. <ref>{{Cite web|url=http://jp.taiwantoday.tw/news.php?unit=151&post=115869|title=TAIWAN TODAY日本語版からのお知らせ|publisher=Taiwan Today|accessdate=2017-05-26|qq=}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 5 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[hak:5-ngie̍t 5-ngit (nùng-la̍k)]][[ja:5月5日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 5일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 5 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月初五]][[zh:五月初五]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839531]] Wikidata name: 5th day of the 5th month in the Chinese lunar calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|May 5|qq=May 5}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月初五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 5일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 5''' is the 5th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Gentoku|Gendoku]] 3rd year / [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1331|1331]] {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 10|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – In the rebellion known as [[:en:Genkō War|Genkō no Ran]], the reattempted anti-bakufu plot of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]] was leaked to the Ashikaga Shogunate, resulting in the arrest of figures such as {{ill|Hino Toshiki|de|日野俊基|ja|日野俊基|zh|日野俊基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Toshiki|1=Hino Toshimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510567}}. The emperor then took refuge on 【Oki Province†Oki_Province】. * [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1615|1615]] {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] set out from Kyoto (from Nijō Castle) in preparation for the summer siege of Osaka. * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenmei]] 6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1786|1786]] June 1) – Under orders from the Tokugawa shogunate, the official [[:en:Mogami Tokunai|Mogami Tokunai]] landed on Ezo (specifically, on Etoroshima, also known as Etoroshima in Oki Province). == Births == * [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 9th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:867|867]] {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 10|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Emperor Uda|Uta Tennō]], the 59th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:931|931]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – {{ill|Uesugi Sadakatsu|de|上杉定勝|ja|上杉定勝|zh|上杉定勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uesugi Sadakatsu|1=Uesugi Sadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514762}}, the 2nd lord of the Yonezawa Domain (died [[:en:1645|1645]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1642|1642]] {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – {{ill|Yamaguchi Sodo|de|山口素堂|ja|山口素堂|zh|山口素堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Sodo|1=Yamaguchi Sodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467412}}, a haiku poet (died [[:en:1716|1716]]). * [[:en:Enkyō (Edo period)|Enkyō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1746|1746]] {{ill|June 23|de|June 23|ja|June 23|zh|June 23|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hanawa Hokiichi|Hanawa Hokeiichi]], a scholar of Japanese literature (died [[:en:1821|1821]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 13rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1763|1763]] {{ill|June 15|de|June 15|ja|June 15|zh|June 15|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 15|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Kobayashi Issa|Kobayashi Issa]], a haiku poet (died [[:en:1827|1827]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunka]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1804|1804]] {{ill|June 12|de|June 12|ja|June 12|zh|June 12|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 12|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Takano Chōei|Takano Nagahide]], a Rangaku scholar (died [[:en:1850|1850]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunka]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1828|1828]] {{ill|June 16|de|June 16|ja|June 16|zh|June 16|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 16|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Saihei Hirose|Hirose Sahei]], a businessperson (died [[:en:1914|1914]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunka]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] {{ill|June 6|de|June 6|ja|June 6|zh|June 6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hon'inbō Shūsaku|Honinbō Shūsaku]], a professional Go player (died [[:en:1862|1862]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1835|1835]] {{ill|May 31|de|May 31|ja|May 31|zh|May 31|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 31|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hijikata Toshizō|Hijikata Toshizō]], vice-commander of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1869|1869]]). == Deaths == * [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1680|1680]] {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Gahō]] (Shunsai), a Zhu Xi scholar and son of Hayashi Rinsan (born [[:en:1618|1618]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] {{ill|June 8|de|June 8|ja|June 8|zh|June 8|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Reizei Tamechika|Reisui Iguyasu]], an imperial court painter. == Commemorations == * [[:en:Dragon Boat Festival|Dragon Boat Festival]] – The Dragon Boat Festival is celebrated. * Duanwu Festival (端午節) is observed in Taiwan. <ref>{{Cite web|url=http://jp.taiwantoday.tw/news.php?unit=151&post=115869|title=TAIWAN TODAY日本語版からのお知らせ|publisher=Taiwan Today|accessdate=2017-05-26|qq=}}</ref>

The emperor then took refuge on 【Oki Province†Oki_Province】. * [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1615|1615]] {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] set out from Kyoto (from Nijō Castle) in preparation for the summer siege of Osaka. * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenmei]] 6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1786|1786]] June 1) – Under orders from the Tokugawa shogunate, the official [[:en:Mogami Tokunai|Mogami Tokunai]] landed on Ezo (specifically, on Etoroshima, also known as Etoroshima in Oki Province). == Births == * [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 9th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:867|867]] {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 10|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Emperor Uda|Uta Tennō]], the 59th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:931|931]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – {{ill|Uesugi Sadakatsu|de|上杉定勝|ja|上杉定勝|zh|上杉定勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uesugi Sadakatsu|1=Uesugi Sadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514762}}, the 2nd lord of the Yonezawa Domain (died [[:en:1645|1645]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1642|1642]] {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – {{ill|Yamaguchi Sodo|de|山口素堂|ja|山口素堂|zh|山口素堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Sodo|1=Yamaguchi Sodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467412}}, a haiku poet (died [[:en:1716|1716]]). * [[:en:Enkyō (Edo period)|Enkyō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1746|1746]] {{ill|June 23|de|June 23|ja|June 23|zh|June 23|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hanawa Hokiichi|Hanawa Hokeiichi]], a scholar of Japanese literature (died [[:en:1821|1821]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 13rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1763|1763]] {{ill|June 15|de|June 15|ja|June 15|zh|June 15|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 15|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Kobayashi Issa|Kobayashi Issa]], a haiku poet (died [[:en:1827|1827]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunka]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1804|1804]] {{ill|June 12|de|June 12|ja|June 12|zh|June 12|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 12|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Takano Chōei|Takano Nagahide]], a Rangaku scholar (died [[:en:1850|1850]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunka]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1828|1828]] {{ill|June 16|de|June 16|ja|June 16|zh|June 16|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 16|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Saihei Hirose|Hirose Sahei]], a businessperson (died [[:en:1914|1914]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunka]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] {{ill|June 6|de|June 6|ja|June 6|zh|June 6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hon'inbō Shūsaku|Honinbō Shūsaku]], a professional Go player (died [[:en:1862|1862]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1835|1835]] {{ill|May 31|de|May 31|ja|May 31|zh|May 31|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 31|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hijikata Toshizō|Hijikata Toshizō]], vice-commander of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1869|1869]]). == Deaths == * [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1680|1680]] {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Gahō]] (Shunsai), a Zhu Xi scholar and son of Hayashi Rinsan (born [[:en:1618|1618]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] {{ill|June 8|de|June 8|ja|June 8|zh|June 8|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Reizei Tamechika|Reisui Iguyasu]], an imperial court painter. == Commemorations == * [[:en:Dragon Boat Festival|Dragon Boat Festival]] – The Dragon Boat Festival is celebrated. * Duanwu Festival (端午節) is observed in Taiwan. <ref>{{Cite web|url=http://jp.taiwantoday.tw/news.php?unit=151&post=115869|title=TAIWAN TODAY日本語版からのお知らせ|publisher=Taiwan Today|accessdate=2017-05-26|qq=}}</ref>

The emperor then took refuge on 【Oki Province†Oki_Province】. * [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1615|1615]] {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June [[:en:Tenpō|Tenmei]] 6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1786|1786]] June 1) – Under orders from the Tokugawa shogunate, the official [[:en:Mogami Tokunai|Mogami Tokunai]] == Events == * [[:en:Gentoku|Gendoku]] 3rd year / [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1331|1331]] {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 10|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – In the rebellion known as [[:en:Genkō War|Genkō no Ran]], the reattempted anti-bakufu plot of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]] was leaked to the Ashikaga Shogunate, resulting in the arrest of figures such as {{ill|Hino Toshiki|de|日野俊基|ja|日野俊基|zh|日野俊基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Toshiki|1=Hino Toshimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510567}}. The emperor then took refuge on 【Oki Province†Oki_Province】. * [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1615|1615]] {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] set out from Kyoto (from Nijō Castle) in preparation for the summer siege of Osaka. * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenmei]] 6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1786|1786]] June 1) – Under orders from the Tokugawa shogunate, the official [[:en:Mogami Tokunai|Mogami Tokunai]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 5 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[hak:5-ngie̍t 5-ngit (nùng-la̍k)]][[ja:5月5日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 5일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 5 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月初五]][[zh:五月初五]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839531]] Wikidata name: 5th day of the 5th month in the Chinese lunar calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|May 5|qq=May 5}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月初五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 5일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 5''' is the 5th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Gentoku|Gendoku]] 3rd year / [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1331|1331]] {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 10|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – In the rebellion known as [[:en:Genkō War|Genkō no Ran]], the reattempted anti-bakufu plot of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]] was leaked to the Ashikaga Shogunate, resulting in the arrest of figures such as {{ill|Hino Toshiki|de|日野俊基|ja|日野俊基|zh|日野俊基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Toshiki|1=Hino Toshimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510567}}. The emperor then took refuge on 【Oki Province†Oki_Province】. * [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1615|1615]] {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] set out from Kyoto (from Nijō Castle) in preparation for the summer siege of Osaka. * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenmei]] 6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1786|1786]] June 1) – Under orders from the Tokugawa shogunate, the official [[:en:Mogami Tokunai|Mogami Tokunai]] landed on Ezo (specifically, on Etoroshima, also known as Etoroshima in Oki Province). == Births == * [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 9th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:867|867]] {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 10|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Emperor Uda|Uta Tennō]], the 59th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:931|931]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – {{ill|Uesugi Sadakatsu|de|上杉定勝|ja|上杉定勝|zh|上杉定勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uesugi Sadakatsu|1=Uesugi Sadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514762}}, the 2nd lord of the Yonezawa Domain (died [[:en:1645|1645]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1642|1642]] {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – {{ill|Yamaguchi Sodo|de|山口素堂|ja|山口素堂|zh|山口素堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Sodo|1=Yamaguchi Sodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467412}}, a haiku poet (died [[:en:1716|1716]]). * [[:en:Enkyō (Edo period)|Enkyō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1746|1746]] {{ill|June 23|de|June 23|ja|June 23|zh|June 23|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hanawa Hokiichi|Hanawa Hokeiichi]], a scholar of Japanese literature (died [[:en:1821|1821]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 13rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1763|1763]] {{ill|June 15|de|June 15|ja|June 15|zh|June 15|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 15|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Kobayashi Issa|Kobayashi Issa]], a haiku poet (died [[:en:1827|1827]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunka]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1804|1804]] {{ill|June 12|de|June 12|ja|June 12|zh|June 12|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 12|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Takano Chōei|Takano Nagahide]], a Rangaku scholar (died [[:en:1850|1850]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunka]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1828|1828]] {{ill|June 16|de|June 16|ja|June 16|zh|June 16|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 16|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Saihei Hirose|Hirose Sahei]], a businessperson (died [[:en:1914|1914]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunka]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] {{ill|June 6|de|June 6|ja|June 6|zh|June 6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hon'inbō Shūsaku|Honinbō Shūsaku]], a professional Go player (died [[:en:1862|1862]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1835|1835]] {{ill|May 31|de|May 31|ja|May 31|zh|May 31|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 31|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hijikata Toshizō|Hijikata Toshizō]], vice-commander of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1869|1869]]). == Deaths == * [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1680|1680]] {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Gahō]] (Shunsai), a Zhu Xi scholar and son of Hayashi Rinsan (born [[:en:1618|1618]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] {{ill|June 8|de|June 8|ja|June 8|zh|June 8|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Reizei Tamechika|Reisui Iguyasu]], an imperial court painter. == Commemorations == * [[:en:Dragon Boat Festival|Dragon Boat Festival]] – The Dragon Boat Festival is celebrated. * Duanwu Festival (端午節) is observed in Taiwan. <ref>{{Cite web|url=http://jp.taiwantoday.tw/news.php?unit=151&post=115869|title=TAIWAN TODAY日本語版からのお知らせ|publisher=Taiwan Today|accessdate=2017-05-26|qq=}}</ref>

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月初五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 5일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 5''' is the 5th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Gentoku|Gendoku]] 3rd year / [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1331|1331]] {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 10|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – In the rebellion known as [[:en:Genkō War|Genkō no Ran]], the reattempted anti-bakufu plot of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]] was leaked to the Ashikaga Shogunate, resulting in the arrest of figures such as {{ill|Hino Toshiki|de|日野俊基|ja|日野俊基|zh|日野俊基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Toshiki|1=Hino Toshimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510567}}. The emperor then took refuge on 【Oki Province†Oki_Province】. * [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1615|1615]] {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] set out from Kyoto (from Nijō Castle) in preparation for the summer siege of Osaka. * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenmei]] 6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1786|1786]] June 1) – Under orders from the Tokugawa shogunate, the official [[:en:Mogami Tokunai|Mogami Tokunai]] landed on Ezo (specifically, on Etoroshima, also known as Etoroshima in Oki Province). == Births == * [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 9th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:867|867]] {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 10|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Emperor Uda|Uta Tennō]], the 59th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:931|931]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – {{ill|Uesugi Sadakatsu|de|上杉定勝|ja|上杉定勝|zh|上杉定勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uesugi Sadakatsu|1=Uesugi Sadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514762}}, the 2nd lord of the Yonezawa Domain (died [[:en:1645|1645]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1642|1642]] {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – {{ill|Yamaguchi Sodo|de|山口素堂|ja|山口素堂|zh|山口素堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Sodo|1=Yamaguchi Sodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467412}}, a haiku poet (died [[:en:1716|1716]]). * [[:en:Enkyō (Edo period)|Enkyō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1746|1746]] {{ill|June 23|de|June 23|ja|June 23|zh|June 23|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 23|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hanawa Hokiichi|Hanawa Hokeiichi]], a scholar of Japanese literature (died [[:en:1821|1821]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 13rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1763|1763]] {{ill|June 15|de|June 15|ja|June 15|zh|June 15|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 15|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Kobayashi Issa|Kobayashi Issa]], a haiku poet (died [[:en:1827|1827]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunka]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1804|1804]] {{ill|June 12|de|June 12|ja|June 12|zh|June 12|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 12|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Takano Chōei|Takano Nagahide]], a Rangaku scholar (died [[:en:1850|1850]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunka]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1828|1828]] {{ill|June 16|de|June 16|ja|June 16|zh|June 16|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 16|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Saihei Hirose|Hirose Sahei]], a businessperson (died [[:en:1914|1914]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunka]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1829|1829]] {{ill|June 6|de|June 6|ja|June 6|zh|June 6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hon'inbō Shūsaku|Honinbō Shūsaku]], a professional Go player (died [[:en:1862|1862]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1835|1835]] {{ill|May 31|de|May 31|ja|May 31|zh|May 31|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 31|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hijikata Toshizō|Hijikata Toshizō]], vice-commander of the Shinsengumi (died [[:en:1869|1869]]). == Deaths == * [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1680|1680]] {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Gahō]] (Shunsai), a Zhu Xi scholar and son of Hayashi Rinsan (born [[:en:1618|1618]]). * [[:en:Genji era|Genji]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1864|1864]] {{ill|June 8|de|June 8|ja|June 8|zh|June 8|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Reizei Tamechika|Reisui Iguyasu]], an imperial court painter. == Commemorations == * [[:en:Dragon Boat Festival|Dragon Boat Festival]] – The Dragon Boat Festival is celebrated. * Duanwu Festival (端午節) is observed in Taiwan. <ref>{{Cite web|url=http://jp.taiwantoday.tw/news.php?unit=151&post=115869|title=TAIWAN TODAY日本語版からのお知らせ|publisher=Taiwan Today|accessdate=2017-05-26|qq=}}</ref>

== Events == * [[:en:Gentoku|Gendoku]] 3rd year / [[:en:Genkō (1331–1334)|Genkō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1331|1331]] {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 10|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – In the rebellion known as [[:en:Genkō War|Genkō no Ran]], the reattempted anti-bakufu plot of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]] was leaked to the Ashikaga Shogunate, resulting in the arrest of figures such as {{ill|Hino Toshiki|de|日野俊基|ja|日野俊基|zh|日野俊基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Toshiki|1=Hino Toshimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510567}}. The emperor then took refuge on 【Oki Province†Oki_Province】. * [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] 1st year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1615|1615]] {{ill|June 1|de|June 1|ja|June 1|zh|June 1|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] set out from Kyoto (from Nijō Castle) in preparation for the summer siege of Osaka. * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenmei]] 6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1786|1786]] June 1) – Under orders from the Tokugawa shogunate, the official [[:en:Mogami Tokunai|Mogami Tokunai]] landed on Ezo (specifically, on Etoroshima, also known as Etoroshima in Oki Province). == Births == * [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 9th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:867|867]] {{ill|June 10|de|June 10|ja|June 10|zh|June 10|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 10|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – [[:en:Emperor Uda|Uta Tennō]], the 59th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:931|931]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] {{ill|June 2|de|June 2|ja|June 2|zh|June 2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – {{ill|Uesugi Sadakatsu|de|上杉定勝|ja|上杉定勝|zh|上杉定勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uesugi Sadakatsu|1=Uesugi Sadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514762}}, the 2nd lord of the
